The Scandinavian Christmas Julbock symbolizes the goat, which is a part of the Yule Goat tradition. The Yule Goat, also known as the Joulupukki, Julbock, Julbukk, Klapparbock, and Yule Straw, is a staple Christmas decoration in Norway and Sweden. It is believed to have originated in pre-Christian Yule celebrations and is associated with the god Thor, whose companion animal was the goat. In medieval times, the Yule goat was used as a costume for masquerading and was associated with the Devil. In the eighteenth century, the goat adopted a new Christmas role in Sweden, becoming a gift bringer.
